Also affects templates/uat/references/${PLATFORM}-pre-flight.html and templates/pipelines/release/<host>/${PLATFORM}.yml. See bug below.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh platform-name mismatch: 'mcp_server' (underscore) vs framework files 'mcp-server' (hyphen)
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh --non-interactive --project x --platform mcp_server --deployment personal --language python --project-dir $WORKDIR/x --allow-existing-dir; observe init log warns 'UAT reference files not found for platform "mcp_server"' and 'No platform module for "mcp_server"'. Files in framework are docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md, templates/uat/references/mcp-server-pre-flight.html, templates/uat/references/mcp-server-scenario.json, templates/pipelines/release/<host>/mcp-server.yml.
framework_bugs.expected: Platform module, UAT references, and (with non-other host) release pipeline templates copied for mcp_server platform; verify-install.sh does not flag missing platform module.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh's $PLATFORM value is 'mcp_server', so $SCRIPT_DIR/docs/platform-modules/${PLATFORM}.md and $SCRIPT_DIR/templates/uat/references/${PLATFORM}-pre-flight.html etc. all miss. Three artifacts silently skipped. verify-install.sh subsequently reports 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing'. Affects every mcp_server platform initialization. Either rename framework files to mcp_server.* or add a normalization layer in init.sh (init.sh:1108-1126, init.sh:2310).

framework_bugs.summary: Generate_release() correctly skips for --git-host other (test assertion miscalibrated)
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh --git-host other --remote-url ... → no .github/workflows/release.yml is created. init.sh:2308 short-circuits: '[ "$host" = "other" ] && return 0'.
framework_bugs.expected: Either init creates release.yml for host=other, or runbook assertion 'release_workflow_created' should be marked N/A when host=other.
framework_bugs.actual: release.yml absent by design. Reported as documentation_gap rather than bug — runbook should clarify.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section A test 'release_workflow_created' does not account for --git-host other path which legitimately skips release pipeline generation. Either runbook should note this exemption or add a test mode flag to init.sh.
init.sh prints 'Release pipeline created at .github/workflows/release.yml' line is omitted for host=other, but Next Steps still show 'Configure release pipeline' implicitly. Mild incoherence.

framework_bugs.actual: current_phase stays at 0; the framework requires manual JSON editing. Same symptom as 2026-04-25 sweep U-D bug. Still open.

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: BL-006 git commit-msg hook still missing — direct shell commit bypasses build-loop check
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a fresh project with .build_loop.feature=null and .features_since_last_test < grace, run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop'` directly in shell. Commit succeeds.
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should block feat: commits with no recorded build loop, regardless of whether the commit is initiated via PreToolUse hook (agent path) or direct git CLI (human path). A git commit-msg hook would close this gap.
framework_bugs.actual: Only the PreToolUse hook fires; no commit-msg hook is installed by init.sh. Direct git commits proceed. Same as 2026-04-25 sweep U-C bug. Still open.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh blocks Phase 3→4 with FAIL even after pen-test artifact is provided. Root cause: pipefail interaction with ls multi-glob (see bug below).

framework_bugs.summary: check-phase-gate.sh pen-test detection fails under pipefail when only one of three glob patterns matches
framework_bugs.reproduction: cd PROJECT_DIR; touch docs/test-results/2026-04-26_pen-test.json; bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/check-phase-gate.sh — output: '[FAIL] Phase 3→4: Full Track requires penetration test'. The check at scripts/check-phase-gate.sh:449 is `if ls docs/test-results/*pen-test* docs/test-results/*pentest* docs/test-results/*penetration* 2>/dev/null | head -1 >/dev/null 2>&1` — script header has `set -euo pipefail`. When unmatched globs fall through (bash default), `ls` is invoked with literal glob args and exits non-zero, which under pipefail propagates up so the if-condition becomes false even though one match is in the output stream.
framework_bugs.expected: If at least one pen-test artifact exists, the gate reports OK.
framework_bugs.actual: Gate FAILS even with valid pen-test artifact, blocking full-track production projects from advancing through Phase 3 → 4. Workaround: include all three substrings in one filename. Fix options: use `find docs/test-results -maxdepth 1 \( -name '*pen-test*' -o -name '*pentest*' -o -name '*penetration*' \) | head -1 | grep -q .` or wrap the ls call in `( set +o pipefail; ls ... )`.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: BUG: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 fails with 'Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode' because init.sh writes poc_mode='production' for --gov-mode production, and start_phase4 blocks any non-null poc_mode. See bug below. Workaround: jq '.poc_mode = null' .claude/phase-state.json then re-run.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: After workaround, all 6 PHASE4_STEPS (production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested) complete sequentially with exit 0 with stub artifacts (HANDOFF.md, RELEASE_NOTES.md, *_handoff*, *_rollback*).

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: handoff_written step's artifact check fails when HANDOFF.md absent; passes once HANDOFF.md is stubbed.

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: go_live_verified step's artifact check fails when RELEASE_NOTES.md absent; passes once stubbed.

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase = 4 (advanced manually due to U-D).

framework_bugs.summary: start_phase4 blocks production scenario: init.sh writes poc_mode='production' but start_phase4 blocks any non-null poc_mode
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh --non-interactive --deployment organizational --gov-mode production ... → .claude/phase-state.json .poc_mode='production'. Later: bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 → exit 1 with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode.'
framework_bugs.expected: Phase 4 should be permitted when gov_mode=production (it's the unlocked state). Only private_poc and sponsored_poc should block Phase 4.
framework_bugs.actual: process-checklist.sh:540 checks `if [ -n "$poc_mode" ] && [ "$poc_mode" != "null" ]` — this rejects any non-null value. Either init.sh should write null for production gov-mode (matching the original poc_mode semantic of 'is this a POC'), or start_phase4 should explicitly compare against 'private_poc' and 'sponsored_poc' only. Without a fix, every production-mode project is blocked from Phase 4 entry. Likely a regression introduced when --gov-mode was added to init.sh as a non-interactive flag (PR #19) without harmonizing the downstream consumer in process-checklist.sh.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only the pre-existing .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ entries that were present at setup; HEAD unchanged at d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494.

summary: Found 1 Critical bug (start_phase4 blocks production-mode projects due to poc_mode='production' value mismatch with process-checklist.sh:540), 2 High bugs (mcp_server platform name mismatch causes silent skip of platform module + UAT references; check-phase-gate.sh pen-test detection fails under pipefail with multi-glob ls), 2 Medium bugs (U-C and U-D confirmations from prior sweep), and 2 documentation gaps. Most PR #17/#18/#19 fixes verified working: pending_approval/lint_uat_scenarios helpers copied, framework-self guard active, BL-006 grace window resets, --non-interactive completes without abort on fake-remote push.
